<zone>

Zone: LLM Analysis Engine (`packages-rex:llm-analysis-engine`)
Files: 28, Cohesion: 0.38, Coupling: 0.62
Risk: critical (score: 0.62)
Description: Orchestrates LLM-driven PRD analysis: bridges raw scan output to the LLM, validates and reasons about proposed modifications, and guards against unsafe consolidations.
Entry points: packages/rex/src/analyze/analyze-shared.ts, packages/rex/src/analyze/consolidation-guard.ts, packages/rex/src/analyze/file-validation.ts, packages/rex/src/analyze/index.ts, packages/rex/src/analyze/llm-bridge.ts, packages/rex/src/analyze/modify-reason.ts, packages/rex/src/analyze/reason.ts, packages/rex/src/analyze/validate-modification.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/commands/analyze.ts, packages/rex/src/cli/commands/format-loe.ts, packages/rex/src/store/atomic-write.ts, packages/rex/src/store/project-config.ts

<findings>

[suggestion] [warning] Zone "LLM Analysis Engine" (packages-rex:llm-analysis-engine) has critical risk (score: 0.62, cohesion: 0.38, coupling: 0.62) — requires refactoring before new feature development

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.38) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.62) — 27 imports target "prd-proposal-pipeline"
- 12 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- The llm-bridge.ts file is the critical seam between scan data and LLM calls — it should be the only file in this zone that constructs LLM prompts, keeping prompt engineering centralized
- consolidation-guard.ts and validate-modification.ts suggest defensive logic for preventing unsafe AI-proposed changes; these are high-value files worth unit testing exhaustively
- The presence of store/atomic-write.ts and store/project-config.ts in this zone (rather than the store zone) indicates the community detection pulled config-writing concerns into the analysis zone — consider whether these store files should be pinned to the store zone
- Cohesion of 0.38 is below the 0.4 threshold; store/atomic-write.ts and store/project-config.ts appear to be misassigned here based on their functional role in the store layer.
- Coupling of 0.62 exceeds the warning threshold; 26 inbound imports from the cli zone and 27 outbound imports to the proposal pipeline zone make this a high-centrality node — changes here have broad blast radius.
- reason.ts and modify-reason.ts together handle rationale tracking for PRD modifications; ensure these are used consistently across all write paths to maintain audit integrity.
- Zone "llm-analysis-engine" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- The zone name 'llm-analysis-engine' implies it drives or owns the analysis, but its 27 outbound imports to prd-proposal-pipeline mean it is actually a consumer of the proposal pipeline rather than its producer — the naming convention inverts the real dependency direction and will mislead developers trying to understand data flow.
- store/atomic-write.ts and store/project-config.ts being pulled into this zone by community detection creates a hidden dependency path: llm-analysis-engine → store internals, bypassing the store zone's intended boundary and making atomic-write available to analysis code without going through a gateway.
- llm-analysis-engine → prd-proposal-pipeline has 27 imports while prd-proposal-pipeline → llm-analysis-engine has 13 — the heavier flow goes from 'engine' into 'pipeline', which is semantically backwards. The engine consumes the pipeline more than the pipeline consumes the engine, suggesting the zone names should be swapped or the boundary redrawn so the pipeline drives the engine.
- store/atomic-write.ts being assigned to llm-analysis-engine by community detection creates a covert cross-zone dependency: analysis code can reach store internals without any gateway indirection. If atomic-write is pinned back to the store zone, any analysis file currently importing it directly will create an undeclared cross-zone import that bypasses the gateway pattern.
- The 5 reverse imports from llm-analysis-engine into cli-mcp-interface create a compile-time dependency from the analysis engine on CLI infrastructure — any change to CLI types or command registration propagates into analysis engine compilation, preventing the engine from being built or tested independently of the CLI layer
- llm-analysis-engine imports from cli-mcp-interface (5 imports) — an analysis engine module depending on CLI-layer code is a tier inversion; the shared symbols should be extracted into a foundation or schema module that both zones consume, eliminating the reverse edge without requiring a broad refactor of either zone
- store/atomic-write.ts being in this zone means any consumer of llm-analysis-engine that imports atomic-write directly will silently bypass the store zone's intended boundary — but there is no automated check that prevents this; the gateway enforcement tests only cover cross-package imports, leaving intra-package zone bypass completely undetected
- The domain-isolation.test.js gateway enforcement covers cross-package boundaries but not intra-package cross-zone direct imports (e.g. analyze files importing store/atomic-write.ts directly); add an intra-package zone-boundary lint rule or test assertion to catch these silent bypasses
- [call graph] 816 internal calls, 69 outgoing, 101 incoming (cohesion: 0.92, coupling: 0.08)

</insights>
